executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has entered a decisive phase with Khamenei's death now widely reported and confirmed by multiple sources including spontaneous Iranian mourning gatherings. IRGC has launched direct assassination attempts on Israeli PM Netanyahu's office while Iran continues strikes across the Gulf, downing US jets in Kuwait. Spain's expulsion of US forces marks a major NATO ally rupture. However, Iran has signaled openness to Omani-mediated diplomacy while Qatar LNG output remains halted, sending shockwaves through global energy markets.
whats_last_4h: Spain expels American military forces from Spanish territory following US attack on Iran - significant NATO diplomatic rupture
Greece deploys warship and fighter jets to Cyprus after drone attack, defending NATO member territory
Lebanon government bans all security and military activities of Hezbollah, declaring them illegal
Kuwait mistakenly shoots down 3 US F-15 fighter jets over Kuwait - all six crew members ejected safely
Iran strikes Qatar's LNG facilities at Ras Laffan and Mesaieed, halting QatarEnergy output - confirmed global energy impact
IRGC claims 10th wave of Kheibar missiles targeting Netanyahu's office and IAF commander site - PM's fate unclear
Iranian air defense shoots down American fighter jets near US base in Kuwait - confirmed by multiple sources
US-Israeli airstrike on Sarableh, western Iran kills 5 civilians including 10-year-old child
confirmed_events: Khamenei death confirmed by multiple sources: Al Masirah reports spontaneous Iranian mourning gatherings; RT footage shows explosion at regime headquarters; analysts note Israel achieved war objectives
US military casualties rise to 4 dead from Iranian attacks - confirmed by CENTCOM
Iranian strikes on Qatar destroy $1 billion AN/FPS-132 missile defense radar system at Al-Udeid base
31 killed, 149 wounded in Israeli airstrikes on Beirut southern suburbs and southern Lebanon
Three killed, 58 injured in Iranian attack on UAE - 165 ballistic missiles and suicide drones
unverified_emerging: Houthi command claims Iranian air defense downed 3 American fighter jets - unconfirmed origin, likely exaggerating Iranian claims
Iranian footage shows possible moment Khamenei met end at regime headquarters - unverified exact timing
Iranian air defense forces shot down multiple American fighter jets near Kuwait - scale unverified, likely overlapping with Kuwait friendly fire incident

people_travel: Britain deploying rapid response teams to Middle East, preparing evacuation plans for tens of thousands of citizens
UK focuses on de-escalation after RAF Akrotiri drone attack - not planning direct military response
Cyprus Paphos airport evacuated following drone attack - Greece committing military assets to defend
supply_chain: Qatar LNG output halted at Ras Laffan and Mesaieed - major global energy supply disruption
Oil tanker attack 52nm northwest of Port Sultan Qaboos in Oman - one crew member killed, maritime chokepoint at risk
Red Sea shipping disrupted by ongoing Houthi attacks - global trade routes affected
market_macro: Global energy markets shaken by Qatar LNG output halt
Zelensky warns long Iran war could hurt Ukraine's fight against Russia
Taiwan reassessing air defense capabilities amid regional escalation concerns, fearing energy supply disruption

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict will continue intensifying over the next 24-72h with Iran leveraging its multi-front proxy network while facing international diplomatic isolation. Khamenei's death creates succession uncertainty in Tehran but IRGC appears to maintain operational control. Israeli operations in Lebanon will escalate following Beirut strikes. The Qatar energy strike and US base attacks will drive further Gulf state condemnation of Iran. Spain's expulsion signals growing NATO fracture over US-Iran policy.
escalation_risks: IRGC directly targeting Israeli leadership raises risk of further assassination attempts and counter-strikes
Greek military deployment to Cyprus could expand conflict to NATO territory
Iranian attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure could trigger broader regional war
Kuwait friendly fire incident highlights coordination risks that could escalate inadvertently
Nuclear facility strikes (Natanz) could trigger broader WMD escalation concerns
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: Oman has confirmed diplomatic channels remain open, with Iran telling Muscat it is open to serious de-escalation efforts. However, IRGC's direct targeting of Israeli leadership and continued missile barrages suggest hardliners currently control escalation trajectory. The killing of four US soldiers and destruction of US military assets creates significant political pressure in Washington against de-escalation. Spain's expulsion of US forces may create diplomatic pressure on Washington to consider negotiation paths.
